title: Garlicky Springy Turkish Lamb Stew
description: A comforting, garlicky, and springy Turkish dish featuring sriracha and rest-meat, low in glycemic index, prepared in a terracotta vessel.

ingredients.name: lamb shoulder
ingredients.notes: bone-in lamb shoulder, cut into 2-inch pieces

ingredients.name: garlic
ingredients.notes: minced, 3 cloves for initial browning and 3 cloves for later addition

instructions: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large terracotta pot over medium-high heat. Brown lamb pieces in batches until nicely browned, then transfer to a plate and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium-low, add remaining 1 tbsp olive oil. Add minced garlic and saut2e until fragrant.
Add browned lamb back to the pot, along with cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in sriracha and lemon juice. Bring to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes, or until the lamb is tender.
Add parsley and cilantro and cook for an additional 2 minutes, allowing the herbs to wilt.
Serve hot, garnished with additional parsley and cilantro if desired, and accompanied by crusty bread or over rice.
Let the stew rest for 10 minutes before serving.
Serve warm, garnished with additional parsley and cilantro if desired, and accompanied by crusty bread or over rice.
